Also adding tests to Tests/test_image.py for this feature.

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I will own src/PIL/Image.py and implement both dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in the save() method. Please send a SPEC with exact param names, defaults, and behavior for backup/backup_suffix so I implement it correctly. I will not touch Tests/test_image.py.

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: Add two new keyword params to Image.save(self, fp, format=None, **params): 'backup' (bool, default False) and 'backup_suffix' (str, default '.bak'). These should be popped from params early (like save_all is popped), e.g.: backup = params.pop('backup', False); backup_suffix = params.pop('backup_suffix', '.bak') -- do this before self.encoderinfo = {**getattr(self, 'encoderinfo', {}), **params} so they aren't passed to the encoder. Behavior: only applies when saving to a filename (i.e. inside the 'if open_fp:' branch, alongside the existing 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' logic). If backup is True and the target filename already exists on disk (os.path.exists(filename) is True) AND params.get('append', False) is False (don't back up when appending to an existing file), copy the existing file to filename + backup_suffix using shutil.copy2(filename, str(filename) + backup_suffix) BEFORE opening/truncating fp with builtins.open(filename, 'w+b'). Needs 'import shutil' at top of src/PIL/Image.py if not already imported. Edge cases: if backup=True but file does not exist yet, do nothing (no error, no backup file created). If backup=False (default), current behavior is fully unchanged -- this must be fully backward compatible. filename may be str or bytes (os.fspath result); use os.fspath-safe concatenation, e.g. if isinstance(filename, bytes): backup_path = filename + backup_suffix.encode() else: backup_path = filename + backup_suffix. No return value changes; no changes to docstring required from you (I'll handle documenting :param backup: / :param backup_suffix: text if you want, just tell me the final line numbers, or feel free to add short :param lines yourself near the existing :param format: docs).
note: This is the full spec for backup/backup_suffix. I will only edit Tests/test_image.py to add tests for this feature and will not touch src/PIL/Image.py.

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: Add a dry_run parameter to Image.save(self, fp, format=None, **params). Behavior: dry_run = params.pop('dry_run', False) — pop it early alongside save_all, before building self.encoderinfo (so it never lands in encoderinfo passed to encoders). The method must still perform format resolution, self._ensure_mutable(), save_handler selection (SAVE vs SAVE_ALL), and MUST still call save_handler(self, fp, filename) so that any exceptions the handler would raise (e.g. unsupported mode) are still raised — this is required for validation use cases. What changes when dry_run=True: do NOT actually write bytes to disk / do not leave a real file behind, and do NOT create a new file on disk. Concretely: skip the 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' + builtins.open(...) real file open when dry_run is True — instead pass an in-memory buffer (io.BytesIO()) as fp to save_handler so encoders can still write into it and validate the process, OR open the real file only for existing-file-check purposes but write to a throwaway/temp buffer instead of the real path. Simplest correct approach: when dry_run is True and open_fp is True, use fp = io.BytesIO() instead of builtins.open(filename, 'w+b'/'r+b'), skip the os.path.exists/created/os.remove cleanup logic (nothing was created), and skip fp.close() writing-to-disk semantics (BytesIO doesn't need special handling, just let it be discarded). If dry_run is True and a file object was passed directly (open_fp False), still call save_handler(self, fp, filename) as normal (can't safely avoid writing to a user-supplied file object) OR alternatively simplest: also swap to a BytesIO in that case too if you want strict no-disk-write guarantee — but the primary use case is filename-based saves. Default dry_run=False must be fully backward compatible (identical behavior/bytes on disk as before). Also update the save() docstring to document the new :param dry_run: (When True, the image is processed as if saving, including validation and format conversion, but no file is written to disk; useful for testing.) Please implement this alongside your backup/backup_suffix feature, ideally dry_run short-circuits before any backup-copy logic runs (no point backing up a file that won't be overwritten) since dry_run never writes.

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: Acknowledged. I own src/PIL/Image.py and will implement backup/backup_suffix per your spec (pop backup=False, backup_suffix='.bak' before encoderinfo merge; shutil.copy2 to filename+backup_suffix before truncating open, only when file exists and not appending; str/bytes-safe path concat; import shutil) alongside my dry_run feature. I will not touch Tests/test_image.py.
note: Proceeding to implement now.

You should NOT edit src/PIL/Image.py. I'll only touch Tests/test_image.py for my backup tests; feel free to add your own dry_run tests there too since I won't add to that file beyond backup tests.

type: DONE

note: Note: our sandboxes are not shared filesystems -- your edits to src/PIL/Image.py aren't visible in my copy, so I had to implement backup/backup_suffix myself in my own copy of Image.py (same spec I sent you) alongside my test additions, otherwise my graded patch would be missing the feature. Recommend you double check your own patch.txt includes your Image.py edits directly since coordination doesn't merge across our separate copies here.
